workspaceNote: This workspace (LTS Stand Alone Software) is a full copy of Development/Business Website/Website Code/v23, including .git and origin remote. v23 itself was not modified. See DECISION_LOG.md.

audit finding F007 (Critical, open): Resend not named as sub-processor in Privacy Policy -- blocks finalizing F007, F058
audit finding F011 (High, open, owner decision): Square terms-gate bypassable via direct link -- relevant to F028
audit finding F016 (High, open): zero automated tests anywhere in the repository -- informs TEST_STRATEGY.md baseline

audit findings F030-F033 (owner decisions): Heroes Discount verification, full-payment vs deposit policy, plan scope, registration model -- block F026-F028, F049-F052, F002 respectively; see OWNER_DECISIONS.md

ownerDecisionsRequired: Primary data store: Netlify Blobs vs managed PostgreSQL (new)
Pricing, discounts, deposits, payment timing (audit F030, F031)
Website Care Plan / Small Business IT plan limits and included work (audit F032)
Customer account registration model: open vs invite-only (audit F033)
Data retention, deletion, backup-aging, legal-hold rules
Legal/privacy/consent wording, including open Critical audit findings F006 and F007
Remote support tooling / device agents / automated website changes scope boundary
filesRequiringReview: docs/development/ARCHITECTURE.md (target architecture proposal, frontend build-step recommendation)
knownRisks: No organization/tenant concept exists in the current codebase at all -- F001 is a from-scratch build, not an extension
Netlify Blobs has no secondary indexes; list()-scan pattern may not scale to Care Hub query needs regardless of which side of the data-store decision is taken
Zero automated tests, zero CI, zero lint/typecheck tooling exist anywhere in the repository today
No payment webhook infrastructure exists at all -- F028 reconciliation starts from zero
Customer documents/images are stored as base64 data URIs in Blobs, not object storage with signed URLs -- does not meet target private-file requirements at scale
Two independent, near-duplicated single-file staff/customer console apps (admin.html, myaccount.html) with repeated auth/fetch boilerplate

F-numbering collision between audit findings (F001-F036) and Business Care Hub functions (F001-F060) -- mitigated via naming convention in DECISION_LOG.md, but any future doc author must remember to disambiguate
